message: beads: close ft-wxqbt (mcp_missions atomic write landed in 149f35d8)

 {"id":"ft-4f1fv","title":"workflows handlers test build misses CompatRuntime import for Runtime::block_on","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:03.547663Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:03.547663Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-58uc0","title":"[gap] README wait-for rule-fire claim not matched by crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs","description":"README.md:144 claims `ft robot wait-for` blocks until a specific rule fires; grep shows the command is defined as "Wait for a pattern in pane output" at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2454-2473 and implemented via `wezterm::compile_wait_matcher` + `PaneWaiter` polling at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:17782-17864.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:08.856774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.645632Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.644794Z","close_reason":"done — README said 'blocks until a specific rule fires' but implementation waits for pattern match in pane output; fixed wording (9f9a7c40)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-5n9my","title":"storage reopen preserves synchronous FTS indexing","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:07:57.132270Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:08:06.383439Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5zyqe","title":"[mock] dataflow.rs:980 merged compute nodes downgraded to NodeKind::Source","description":"dataflow.rs:980 in DataflowGraph::merge() sets `kind: NodeKind::Source, // placeholder` for merged compute/debounce nodes because ComputeFn is not Clone. This silently degrades merged compute nodes to inert Source nodes — their computation logic is lost. Production behavior should be: either (a) add a NodeKind::InertCompute variant so callers can detect and re-register the compute fn, (b) make ComputeFn clonable via Arc wrapper, or (c) log a warning when a compute node is downgraded during merge. The doc comment at line 965-968 acknowledges the limitation and tells callers to re-register, but the silent downgrade is error-prone.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:39.280862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.126666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.062995Z","close_reason":"implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3599,"issue_id":"ft-5zyqe","author":"jemanuel","text":"Commit cef1c892 switches dataflow ComputeFn to Arc so merge() can preserve Compute/Debounce node kinds instead of downgrading them to Source. Updated merge docs, rewired merge() to clone node kinds, and added regression tests proving merged compute nodes still recompute and merged debounce nodes still buffer+flush. Local verification: rustfmt + git diff --check passed; UBS found no critical issues in crates/frankenterm-core/src/dataflow.rs. Requested rch run is still spending the window in full-workspace sync with no compile/test result line yet.","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-61kg4","title":"events::EventDeduplicator::check: max_capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:843-891 EventDeduplicator::check with max_capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. Instead: the novel-key eviction path at line 875 fires (entries.len()=0 >= 0=true), pop_front on empty insertion_order is a no-op, and the insert at line 881-888 still succeeds. Effect on alternating keys A, B, A: each is reported 'New' (because evict-then-insert toggles which key occupies the single slot), but an immediate repeat of the same key returns Duplicate. This is the same 'zero capacity → 1-slot ghost cache' shape fixed in connector_inbound_bridge ft-bx4le (commit 8fdfd114). Fix: early-return DedupeVerdict::New at the top of check when self.max_capacity == 0, matching ft-bx4le's pattern. No state accumulation, consistent 'no dedup' semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:51.129305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572881Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572605Z","close_reason":"Landed in bf854b26 (fix(events): bypass dedup when EventDeduplicator max_capacity=0). Short-circuit DedupeVerdict::New at top of check() when max_capacity == 0, mirroring ft-bx4le's pattern for SignalDeduplicator. Regression test dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_61kg4 pins the behavior. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6269x","title":"[reality-gap] ft snapshot restore Unix-only status is not disclosed in README session-persistence matrix","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:128 Session Persistence matrix row:

    | Session persistence | Supported with backend prerequisite |
    Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and `ft session doctor`
    are implemented, but live restore still depends on the current
    WezTerm mux interop boundary. \`ft restart\` is currently
    Unix-only. |

Disclosed: `ft restart` Unix-only.
Undisclosed: `ft snapshot restore` has the *same* cfg gate.

# Reality (code)

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27100` (ft restart — disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:39715` (ft snapshot restore — NOT disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft snapshot restore is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

Same gate, same failure mode. README line 128 specifically mentions
`ft restart` as the Unix-only surface but omits `ft snapshot restore`,
which is also mentioned in the *same* matrix row ("Snapshots, session
inspection, **restore**, and `ft session doctor` are implemented").

A Windows operator reading the matrix is told "restore is
implemented" (true, on Unix) without the cross-platform caveat.

# Failure shape

Operator on Windows runs `ft snapshot restore ...` → eprintln hits
→ exit 1. Workaround unknown.

# Recommended disposition

Update README.md:128 to say:

    Snapshots and session inspection are implemented cross-platform;
    `ft restart` and `ft snapshot restore` are currently Unix-only.

One-line doc change. Alternative: fix the cfg gate. The session
persistence code under `#[cfg(unix)]` likely depends on real Unix
signals / fork semantics — porting is non-trivial. Docs-first is
the pragmatic path.

# Related

ft-2guux previously flagged G2 "`ft restart` Unix-only not in
support matrix" and was closed 2026-04-21 with a README.md:128
update. That update added disclosure for `ft restart` but missed
`ft snapshot restore` — this bead is the follow-up.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"pane8","owner":"pane3-readme-fix","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:55.113196Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:49:57.208440Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:49:57.208098Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","platform","reality-gap"]}
@@ -233,7 +234,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-wajba","title":"tmux cmd_queue drop-head-in-flight corrupts Guarded response routing","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs:73-91 (push_command_capped) vs :165-186 (Guarded response handler).

push_command_capped(queue, cmd) does queue.drain(..excess) when queue exceeds CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000. The drain is from the FRONT. But when TmuxDomainState.state == WaitingForResponse, the front of cmd_queue is the in-flight command — send_next_command only peeks front() and writes, it does not pop; the pop happens at the Guarded response handler (tmux.rs:172) via cmd_queue.pop_front().

Race / correctness gap: if push_command_capped runs while state == WaitingForResponse and queue.len() reaches 50_001, drain drops the in-flight head. When the Guarded response arrives, pop_front() pops a DIFFERENT pending command and calls cmd.process_result(domain_id, &response) on the wrong command — cross-wiring response payload onto the wrong command parser. Both threads hold the cmd_queue lock so the drain+pop ordering is deterministic under the lock, but the correctness violation stands.

Fix options (not applied here, pattern-level):
 1. When state == WaitingForResponse, skip index 0 in drain: queue.drain(1..=excess) (but preserves head).
 2. Drain from the back (newest-first) which discards most recently enqueued layout/session events that often re-fire.
 3. Abort the in-flight state machine (set state=Idle) and drain — heavy-handed but restores invariant.

Only triggers in pathological event storms (remote tmux lag + 50_000 queued commands) so severity is MEDIUM. The original bug fixed in 1055cffc (push-site cap enforcement) traded unbounded memory for this subtle correctness edge. A regression test would drive tmux into WaitingForResponse, push >50_001 Layout/SessionChanged events, then fire a Guarded response and assert process_result is NOT called against a different command type than the one that was sent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:32.972876Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.760204Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.759836Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 9cc7e6c2. push_command_capped migrated from free function to TmuxDomainState method so it can consult self.state before choosing drain range:
- state == WaitingForResponse → drain(1..=excess) (preserves index 0, the in-flight cmd awaiting its Guarded response)
- any other state → drain(..excess) (retains existing drop-oldest semantics)

Both drain branches log distinctly so event-storm incidents are traceable. Lock ordering matches Guarded-handler's (cmd_queue first, state transiently inside method): no deadlock. send_next_command's cap-drain is untouched — already guarded by early 'state != Idle → return'.

Regression coverage: 2 new tests under mux::tmux::tests that each seat a distinguishable SplitPane at index 0 and push CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH more commands; one flips state to WaitingForResponse and asserts the head's get_command() text is unchanged (pre-fix fails), the other keeps state Idle and asserts the head IS drained (pins against future over-correction). Existing cmd_queue_hard_cap_drains_oldest_when_exceeded still green after the refactor.

Verification via rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target:
- cargo check -p mux --lib: clean
- cargo test -p mux --lib cmd_queue: 3/3 green.

Unrelated pre-existing proptest_pane_serde compile failure (17-tuple exceeds proptest::Strategy impl) blocks --tests build for the whole crate but does not touch the patched code path; flagged for a separate bead if not already filed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wk5fo","title":"move FTS5 indexing off the capture-write transaction","description":"# Background

Follow-up to ft-kcsae (answered). Live capture path in storage.rs
writes synchronously INTO the FTS5 index via three AFTER-triggers:

    storage.rs:169  output_segments_ai AFTER INSERT  → INSERT INTO output_segments_fts
    storage.rs:173  output_segments_ad AFTER DELETE  → fts delete
    storage.rs:177  output_segments_au AFTER UPDATE  → fts delete + insert

Every captured segment pays the inverted-index rebuild cost before
`append_segment_sync` returns (storage.rs:12308 → 12335 INSERT INTO
output_segments). Under 200-pane fanout the trigger cost is paid 200x
per-wire-tick.

# Existing primitives that can be reused

- `fts_pane_progress` table (storage.rs:472) — already tracks per-pane
  last-indexed-seq with resumable offsets.
- `sync_fts_on_startup(conn, FtsSyncConfig)` (storage.rs:16131) — the
  batched catchup engine. Currently only fires at startup.
- `FtsSyncConfig` (storage.rs:2363) — batch_size=100, max_batch_bytes=1
  MB, commit_progress per batch. Ready knobs.

# Proposal

1. Drop the three AFTER triggers on output_segments.
2. Add a writer-thread ticker (every N seconds OR after M segments
   since last catchup) that runs `sync_fts_on_startup` with the
   existing FtsSyncConfig and uses `fts_pane_progress` to pick up
   where it left off.
3. Gate behind a `storage.fts_indexing_mode = 'sync' | 'deferred'`
   config flag; default 'sync' at first, flip to 'deferred' after a
   round of search-freshness observability.
4. Expose catchup-lag (now - max(fts_pane_progress.last_indexed_at))
   as a metric so operators can spot index drift.

# Risk

- Search freshness becomes eventual (default lag = catchup interval).
- FTS rows can briefly diverge from output_segments during a crash
  between INSERT and the next catchup tick — acceptable because
  `sync_fts_on_startup` already handles gap recovery on startup.

# Measurement prerequisite

Before landing: add a bench that measures `append_segment_sync` p99
under a 200-pane load with FTS5 enabled (current) vs. triggers
disabled + deferred catchup. If the gap is not material, don't ship —
the synchronous path is simpler and the trigger tax might be in the
noise given SQLite's WAL + page cache.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane1","owner":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:00:59.588862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206770Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206400Z","close_reason":"Opt-in deferred FTS indexing landed as d74401c6. Added StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers: bool (default false, preserves current sync behavior). When true, StorageHandle::with_config executes DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS on output_segments_a[iud] immediately after initialize_schema, so new segment writes no longer synchronously rebuild FTS inverted-index pages. Callers are responsible for periodic sync_fts() invocation; the fts_pane_progress + sync_fts_on_startup engine already handles resumable batched catchup. Verified via 4 new regression tests in fts_sync_tests (17 total passing): fts_sync_mode_indexes_on_insert (baseline), fts_deferred_mode_does_not_index_on_insert (post-DROP empty FTS), fts_deferred_mode_catchup_indexes_all_segments (500 segments → catchup → 500 indexed, second catchup is no-op), fts_deferred_mode_catchup_resumes_across_rounds (5 rounds × 100 segments interleaved with catchup, each round indexes exactly 100). Surfaced but did NOT fix: pre-existing seq=0 off-by-one in sync_fts_for_pane (WHERE seq > last_indexed_seq excludes fresh pane's seq=0 segment) — tests use seq 1..=N, separate bead for that fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3602,"issue_id":"ft-wk5fo","author":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","text":"Claimed for scaffolding. Build verification currently blocked by ft-h9g0q (non-exhaustive match on new Error variants in error_renderer.rs:34 + retry.rs:378). Just shipped a mechanical fix in commit 87d4546b covering all 3 new typed variants; waiting on cold cargo check to confirm green. Once compile is green, scope for this bead's first shippable slice is the criterion benchmark for append_segment_sync p99 described in the ticket's 'Measurement prerequisite' — no implementation changes before we have numbers.","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-wx5ln","title":"pattern pack subdirectories ignore supported rules.yaml/yml/json manifests","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:44.211855Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:48.216864Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-wxqbt","title":"mcp_missions::mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path: non-atomic write corrupts tx contract on mid-write crash","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_missions.rs:289-308 mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path serializes a MissionTxContract and calls std::fs::write(path, json) directly. fs::write is NOT atomic — it open+truncate+write in a single sequence. If the process crashes (panic, SIGKILL, power loss, disk full mid-write) between truncate and final write, the target file is left truncated or with partial JSON. On next startup mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path reads the partial file, serde_json::from_str bails with 'Invalid tx contract JSON', and the ENTIRE tx contract is lost — not the last update, the whole in-flight contract. Same shape applies to mcp_save_mission_contract_to_path if it exists via a copy-paste. The codebase already has the atomic-rename pattern for durable writes (config_profiles.rs:129-135 manifest writes, recorder_storage.rs:1061, rulesets.rs:182, wal_engine.rs:892). Fix: write to path.with_extension('json.tmp') first, then std::fs::rename to the final path. rename is atomic on POSIX and NTFS — the tx file is either the old contents or the new contents, never a truncated halfway state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:05:53.858137Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:05:53.858137Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-wxqbt","title":"mcp_missions::mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path: non-atomic write corrupts tx contract on mid-write crash","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_missions.rs:289-308 mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path serializes a MissionTxContract and calls std::fs::write(path, json) directly. fs::write is NOT atomic — it open+truncate+write in a single sequence. If the process crashes (panic, SIGKILL, power loss, disk full mid-write) between truncate and final write, the target file is left truncated or with partial JSON. On next startup mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path reads the partial file, serde_json::from_str bails with 'Invalid tx contract JSON', and the ENTIRE tx contract is lost — not the last update, the whole in-flight contract. Same shape applies to mcp_save_mission_contract_to_path if it exists via a copy-paste. The codebase already has the atomic-rename pattern for durable writes (config_profiles.rs:129-135 manifest writes, recorder_storage.rs:1061, rulesets.rs:182, wal_engine.rs:892). Fix: write to path.with_extension('json.tmp') first, then std::fs::rename to the final path. rename is atomic on POSIX and NTFS — the tx file is either the old contents or the new contents, never a truncated halfway state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:05:53.858137Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:08:14.359806Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:08:14.359416Z","close_reason":"Landed in 149f35d8 (fix(mcp_missions): atomic tmp+rename for mission/tx-contract saves). Both mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path and mcp_save_mission_to_path now write to <path>.json.tmp and std::fs::rename onto the target. Two regression tests (save_tx_contract_is_atomic_rename_ft_wxqbt, save_mission_is_atomic_rename_ft_wxqbt) assert target file presence + .tmp-sibling absence + round-trip parseability. Verification of the tests blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core compile blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-x2fvv","title":"[mor:performance-engineering] PaneTierClassifier takes RwLock.write() on every output byte","description":"# Observation

`PaneTierClassifier::on_pane_output` (pane_tiers.rs:268) acquires
\`panes.write()\` on EVERY output notification from EVERY pane, just
to update \`last_output: Instant\` and conditionally bump the tier
field. The crate has 15 \`.write()\` calls on this single RwLock.

In a 200+ pane fleet where many panes are running cargo/npm output
at 10-100 KB/s each, the on_pane_output path turns into a queue at
the RwLock. Every pane's output fans in through a single exclusive
acquisition.

Structural cost:
- fan-in × output rate × RwLock-acquire latency
- when backpressure is shedding (exactly when you want speed), the
  classifier is *more* contended because tier demotions also write

# Question

Two shapes worth considering:

1. Per-pane atomics: replace \`HashMap<u64, PaneState>\` with a
   \`DashMap<u64, Arc<PaneStateAtomic>>\` where \`last_output\` is
   an \`AtomicU64\` (epoch ms) and \`tier\` is an enum-shaped
   \`AtomicU8\`. on_pane_output becomes a load + CAS, never a
   lock. Tier computation still needs coherent reads, which DashMap
   per-shard locks provide without global contention.

2. Batch ingress: on_pane_output doesn't need to update
   synchronously. Drop the event into a bounded SPSC-per-pane, have
   a single classifier task drain them every N ms, and do the map
   mutation off the hot path entirely. Same consistency model as
   backpressure.

Either shape turns the current O(hot-path-per-byte) into something
closer to O(hot-path-per-batch). Worth a flamegraph under a
200-pane workload before picking.

# Hot-path reasoning

This is the classic "correctness by big lock" pattern. It's right
for a single-pane dev tool; it's load-bearing against you when the
identity of the project is multi-pane orchestration. Core substrate
(Named Tmux + 200-pane swarms) implies the classifier's hot path is
the product's hot path.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"question","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:47.955775Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:29.236268Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:29.235933Z","close_reason":"Verified in 0b65bc0b","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hot-path","lock-contention","mor","performance"]}
 {"id":"ft-x4k2b","title":"[reality-gap] runtime_compat 'audited boundary' scope understated — 709 call sites across 83 files","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:38-42:

    The runtime model is asupersync-native in the product-facing
    APIs: structured, cancel-correct, and centered on Cx-aware
    orchestration across search, policy, workflows, robot mode,
    and the feature-gated web/distributed surfaces. The remaining
    watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses a named
    runtime_compat quarantine while the last task-spawn seams are
    being collapsed.

…and:

    `runtime_compat` remains as an audited boundary for runtime
    lifecycle, channel, time, and blocking semantics; it is not
    the product story.

The framing is: small, named, collapsing.

# Reality (code)

    rg -c "runtime_compat::" crates/frankenterm-core/src/
      → 709 call sites across 83 files

The boundary isn't a narrow interop shim — it's the default way
product code spawns tasks, opens channels, sleeps, and times out.
Every capture loop, every workflow step, every web handler, every
mux-pool operation transits it. The "named quarantine" framing
implies 1-2 modules; the code implies every subsystem.

# Why this matters

- **Trust calibration**: operators and contributors use the README
  to calibrate what's "native asupersync" vs. "transitioning." A
  readme that says "the boundary is small" while 83 files sit on
  it gives the wrong picture of where the project is on its own
  milestone (ft-xbnl0).
- **Cancellation semantics**: asupersync's Cx-first path is
  behaviourally different from runtime_compat's tokio fallback
  (see ft-xbnl0 epic for the formal distinction). Callers reading
  the README might assume their task spawns are Cx-aware when
  they're not.
- **Migration progress tracking**: ft-xbnl0.2.x has been landing
  fixes at the task-spawn level for weeks. The README should track
  which specific paths have crossed the threshold and which are
  still on runtime_compat, so operators can calibrate expectations.

# Recommended disposition

Update README.md:38-42 with either a count ("runtime_compat is
still load-bearing for ~700 call sites across 83 files; the
ft-xbnl0 epic tracks collapse"), or a list of the specific
product surfaces still on runtime_compat (watch daemon,
capture loops, workflow steps, web handlers).

Either form gives operators a calibrated picture without
overstating or understating the state.

# Related

- ft-2guux G1 raised the same concern about the watch daemon
  specifically; closed with a README clarification.
- ft-xbnl0 is the open epic tracking the full migration. This bead

 {"id":"ft-x5tx2","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:25:58.707216Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.588832Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.587715Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","stub-audit"]}
